{"key_event":"Emergency and democratic backlash","reasoning":"The Emergency demonstrated near-overwhelming coercive and administrative reach, but the 1977 electoral reversal, 44th Amendment, regional agitations, and 1984 breakdowns showed real limits to state authority and legitimacy. Civil society was badly damaged by censorship, arrests, sterilization drives, and later communal violence, yet post-Emergency elections, press assertiveness, rights activism, agrarian/regional mobilization, and opposition politics kept it strongly able to constrain the state.","society_change":-0.3,"society_power":7.8,"state_change":-0.1,"state_power":9.2}
